evals.name: bootstrap-creates-correct-state
evals.prompt: You are running /web-evolve landing for the first time on a project. The project has src/components/landing/HeroSection.tsx (120 lines) and src/components/landing/PricingTable.tsx (95 lines). No .web-evolve/ directory exists. Describe exactly what state.json you would write after Step 1A bootstrap. Include all fields.
evals.rationale: Tests that Step 1A bootstrap writes all required v2 fields at correct initial values.

evals.name: scope-switch-adds-new-areas
evals.prompt: state.json has last_scope: landing and two landing areas both status: done. User runs /web-evolve /clients. What happens in Step 1 and what does state.json look like after?
expected_assertions: Step 1 bootstraps /clients areas without removing landing areas
session_skill_calls is NOT reset — carries over from prior skill calls in this conversation
evals.rationale: Tests scope-switch mid-project. session_skill_calls must persist across scope switches within same conversation.

evals.name: needs-work-pass-reset
evals.prompt: An area has status: needs-work, pass: 2, score: 67. Fresh session starts, Step 2 picks this area. When Step 5 begins, what is area.pass and why?
expected_assertions: area.pass reset to 0 at start of Step 5
reason references prior session pass being stale
evals.rationale: Tests needs-work re-entry resets pass to 0. Without this, Step 5 immediately exits with zero fix attempts.

evals.name: context-budget-halt-after-scoring
evals.prompt: Skill('clarify') was called earlier this session (session_skill_calls=1). In Step 5, need to call Skill('visual-uplift'). Area scored: Hook:18, Visuals:11, Clarity:21, Function:20 (total 70). What happens and what does state.json look like?
expected_assertions: state: status=needs-work, score=70, dimensions written, pass written
evals.rationale: Tests context budget halt after scoring â€” state must be persisted for next session resume.

evals.name: regression-revert-step6c
evals.prompt: An area scored 71 before fixing. After fix pass 1, re-score gives 65 (lower). What happens? Specifically: which git commands run, what status and pass values are written to state.json, what is the commit message format?
evals.rationale: Tests Step 6 outcome C (regression revert). Critical for preventing scores going backwards undetected.

evals.name: taste-gate-max-iterations
evals.prompt: Area scored 88 (above 85). Taste gate runs, finds banned pattern on pass 1 and pass 2. What happens on the third taste gate check attempt?
expected_assertions: Max 2 iterations enforced via taste_gate_attempts counter
evals.rationale: Tests Step 6D taste gate loop termination. Prevents infinite loop when a fix skill keeps re-introducing banned patterns.

evals.name: zero-match-guard
evals.prompt: Running /web-evolve landing on a Remix project. The landing globs (src/components/landing/, src/components/sections/, etc.) all return zero files. What happens?
expected_assertions: Halt with warning message naming the scope
No areas created for empty scope
evals.rationale: Tests Step 1A zero-match guard. An empty state.json causes Step 2 to report false completion.

evals.name: run-counter-first-vs-rescore
evals.prompt: An area has run: 3, status: needs-work, score: 62. A new session begins, step 2 picks this area, step 4 re-scores it at 71. What are run_counter and area.run after the re-score?
expected_assertions: run_counter NOT incremented (area was previously scored)
Two actions are separate: counter increment only on first-score
evals.rationale: Tests Step 4 run_counter vs area.run distinction. Critical: conflating them causes wrong run numbers.

evals.name: v1-state-migration
evals.prompt: A project has .web-evolve/state.json containing a key "phases" from the old v1 engine. User runs /web-evolve landing. What happens in Step 1?
expected_assertions: Step 1A bootstrap runs to create fresh v2 state
evals.rationale: Tests v1 state migration guard. Without this, v1 state keys (phases, trajectory) cause downstream step failures.

evals.name: malformed-state-json
evals.prompt: state.json exists but contains invalid JSON (truncated mid-object from a prior crash). User runs /web-evolve landing. What happens?
evals.rationale: Tests malformed JSON handling in Step 1. Prevents parse errors from blocking all future runs.
